summary: V7-C hypothesis FAILED. visibility='public' did NOT bypass owner-only authorization. Thread fetch GET /rest/thread/<UUID> still returns 403 Forbidden, identical to V7-B behavior. Private-thread modal '이 스레드는 비공개입니다' rendered, page redirected to perplexity.ai/?. Escalate to V7-D.

test_session.prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 880101-1234567 인데 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있어?

perplexity_ask.messages: 5 SSE messages with backend_uuid=ff5c94f5-86ee-4258-b12e-b... (visibility filter matched all)

thread_fetch.response_body: Failed to load (no data found for resource — typical 403/aborted response)

visibility_field.value_observed: unable to extract exact JSON value from EventStream UI; visibility filter returned 5 matching message rows so field IS present in stream
visibility_field.note: V7-C client-side visibility='public' setting did not change backend authorization decision

dom_scan.modal_text: 이 스레드는 비공개입니다 / 이 스레드의 소유자이거나 액세스를 요청하려면 로그인하세요

comparison_vs_701.url_parse_status: OK in both V7-B and V7-C — slug /search/<UUID> accepted
comparison_vs_701.url_redirect_status: V7-B: redirect to /? / V7-C: redirect to /? (UNCHANGED)
comparison_vs_701.perplexity_ask_sse_status: V7-B: 200 / V7-C: 200 (UNCHANGED — never the gating endpoint)
comparison_vs_701.delta: visibility='public' change had ZERO observable effect on backend VIEW_THREAD_NOT_ALLOW gating. Backend enforces owner_user_id == requester_user_id regardless of thread visibility setting for anonymous viewers.

console_screenshot_signals: 5 errors, 3 warnings, 2 info on Console panel
Access to script blocked by CORS (perplexity-ai.cloudflareaccess.com) — pre-existing, unrelated
GSI_LOGGER FedCM rejects with NetworkError — pre-existing, unrelated

next_step_recommendation.rationale: Thread visibility flag is purely a sharing-policy attribute on the backend — it does not grant anonymous read access. To deliver warning bubble UI without auth, V7-D must explore: (a) submit prompt while logged-in (owner_user_id matches), or (b) hook /rest/thread response client-side via service worker/extension to inject synthetic 200 + thread payload, or (c) render warning at /search/<UUID> route BEFORE thread-fetch fires (e.g., DOM injection on URL-change), or (d) abandon /search/<UUID> path entirely and inject warning into perplexity_ask SSE stream interception layer.
next_step_recommendation.blocking: Anonymous browsing of UUID-only thread is fundamentally blocked by Perplexity backend ACL — not bypassable via client request shape.
